disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Insidious generalised fatigue and weakness over ~3 months with sharp escalation over the last 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: How long have you been feeling this way?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been getting more and more tired for about three months, but the last two days I've gone downhill really fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Two syncopal episodes on standing today, with a strong sense of near-fainting whenever she stands (postural symptoms)
trigger_question_examples: What happens when you stand up?
Have you fainted or nearly fainted?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Twice today I passed out just trying to stand up out of my chair, and every time I stand I feel like I'm going to black out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Very poor dietary intake — mostly tea and toast, no fresh green vegetables
trigger_question_examples: What does a typical day of meals look like for you?
Are you managing to shop and cook?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I just have tea and toast most days. I don't buy or cook fresh vegetables anymore — it's too hard on my own.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sore, smooth, red tongue with cracks at the corners of the mouth, reducing oral intake
trigger_question_examples: Is anything hurting when you eat?
Have you noticed anything about your mouth or tongue?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My tongue has gone smooth and red and it's so sore, and the corners of my mouth are cracked — it hurts too much to eat properly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Denies any pins-and-needles, numbness or tingling in the hands or feet
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness, tingling or pins-and-needles in your hands or feet?
Any trouble feeling your feet or with your balance walking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my hands and feet feel completely normal — no tingling or numbness at all.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so — it's really just the tiredness and my sore mouth.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone, frail, high risk of a traumatic fall from recurrent near-collapse
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone with you at home?
Do you feel safe standing and moving around on your own?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own. My neighbour looks in sometimes, but I'm scared I'll fall and not be able to get up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent medication changes and not on any blood-pressure-lowering medication that would explain the postural symptoms
trigger_question_examples: Have you started or changed any medications recently?
Are you on any tablets for blood pressure?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not really on any regular tablets, and nothing has changed.
